ddls:
CREATE TABLE "hvac_units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each HVAC unit */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the HVAC unit within the building */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the HVAC unit was installed */,
  "capacity" REAL /* Rated capacity of the HVAC unit in kW */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the HVAC unit */,
  "model_number" TEXT /* Model number of the HVAC unit */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the HVAC unit (active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  "building_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the building where the unit is installed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor reading */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the HVAC unit providing the data */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the sensor */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was recorded */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data (e.g., temperature, airflow) */,
  "value" REAL /* Recorded value of the data */,
  "units" TEXT /* Units of the recorded value (e.g., C, L/s) */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (good, suspect, bad) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hvac_units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the data type (e.g., condenser_water_temperature) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the data type represents */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Standard unit of measurement for the data type */,
  "criticality_level" TEXT /* Criticality level of the data type (high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_ranges" (
  "range_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each range entry */,
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data type the range applies to */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum acceptable value for the data type */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum acceptable value for the data type */,
  "tolerance" REAL /* Acceptable tolerance range for the data type */,
  "alert_threshold" REAL /* Threshold for generating alerts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("range_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_ranges_data_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_type_id") REFERENCES data_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "energy_loads" (
  "load_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each energy load entry */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the HVAC unit associated with the load value */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the load was recorded */,
  "load_value" REAL /* Energy load value */,
  "units" TEXT /* Units of the energy load value (e.g., W) */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration for which the load was measured (in minutes) */,
  "peak_load" INTEGER /* Indicates if the recorded load was a peak load */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("load_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_loads_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hvac_units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the HVAC unit for which the alert is generated */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was logged */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., low temperature, high load) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (critical, warning, info) */,
  "resolved_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who resolved the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES hvac_units ("unit_id")
)
